## Imperfect Human Feedback: Inconsistency

The paper demonstrated that systematic integration of time-inconsistency and false beliefs aligned with humans.

They asked humans $(n=130)$ to explain observed behaviors, as well as predicted them with their model.

They find empircally that there was some match in the probability distributions.

**Takeaways**: time-inconsistency and false beliefs are real, systematic ways humans are biased when providing demonstrations. Including these in reward learning models could improve the accuracy of the recovered reward.

[@inconsistent]{style="font-size: 0.8em;"}
